test_cases.natural_language: Find all tracks that have no composer listed
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT t.Name AS track_name FROM Track t WHERE t.Composer IS NULL ORDER BY t.Name LIMIT 20;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Tests NULL handling — models often generate WHERE Composer = '' instead of IS NULL

test_cases.natural_language: Which customers do not have a company?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.Email FROM Customer c WHERE c.Company IS NULL ORDER BY c.LastName;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Another NULL test — 'do not have' should map to IS NULL, not = '' or != something

test_cases.natural_language: List all invoices from the first quarter of 2025
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT InvoiceId, CustomerId, InvoiceDate, Total FROM Invoice WHERE InvoiceDate >= '2025-01-01' AND InvoiceDate < '2025-04-01' ORDER BY InvoiceDate;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Temporal reasoning — model must correctly interpret 'first quarter' as Jan-Mar, handle date boundary

test_cases.natural_language: Show tracks that are longer than 10 minutes and cost more than $0.99
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT t.Name AS track_name, ROUND(t.Milliseconds / 60000.0, 1) AS duration_minutes, t.UnitPrice FROM Track t WHERE t.Milliseconds > 600000 AND t.UnitPrice > 0.99 ORDER BY t.Milliseconds DESC LIMIT 20;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Unit conversion trap — must convert 10 min to 600000 ms, and use > not >= for price

test_cases.natural_language: Find customers whose email is from a gmail address
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT FirstName, LastName, Email FROM Customer WHERE Email LIKE '%gmail.com' ORDER BY LastName;
test_cases.adversarial_note: String pattern matching — model must use LIKE with wildcard, not exact match

test_cases.natural_language: How much revenue has each artist generated from sales?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ar.Name AS artist, ROUND(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 2) AS total_revenue FROM Artist ar JOIN Album a ON ar.ArtistId = a.ArtistId JOIN Track t ON a.AlbumId = t.AlbumId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 15;
test_cases.adversarial_note: 4-table JOIN chain: Artist->Album->Track->InvoiceLine. Models often miss intermediate tables.

test_cases.natural_language: Show the management hierarchy: each employee and who they report to
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.FirstName AS emp_first, e.LastName AS emp_last, e.Title AS emp_title, m.FirstName AS mgr_first, m.LastName AS mgr_last FROM Employee e LEFT JOIN Employee m ON e.ReportsTo = m.EmployeeId ORDER BY e.EmployeeId;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Self-join on Employee table + LEFT JOIN to include top-level (ReportsTo IS NULL)

test_cases.natural_language: Which playlists contain tracks by Led Zeppelin?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT p.Name AS playlist_name FROM Playlist p JOIN PlaylistTrack pt ON p.PlaylistId = pt.PlaylistId JOIN Track t ON pt.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Album a ON t.AlbumId = a.AlbumId JOIN Artist ar ON a.ArtistId = ar.ArtistId WHERE ar.Name = 'Led Zeppelin' ORDER BY p.Name;
test_cases.adversarial_note: 5-table JOIN chain: Playlist->PlaylistTrack->Track->Album->Artist. Also needs DISTINCT.

test_cases.natural_language: List each customer with the total number of tracks they have purchased
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, COUNT(il.InvoiceLineId) AS tracks_purchased FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON i.InvoiceId = il.InvoiceId GROUP BY c.CustomerId ORDER BY tracks_purchased DESC LIMIT 15;
test_cases.adversarial_note: 3-table JOIN: Customer->Invoice->InvoiceLine. Must count InvoiceLine, not Invoice.

test_cases.natural_language: Which customers are handled by employees who were hired before 2003?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, c.Country FROM Customer c JOIN Employee e ON c.SupportRepId = e.EmployeeId WHERE e.HireDate < '2003-01-01' ORDER BY c.LastName LIMIT 20;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Cross-entity filtering — filter on Employee attribute but return Customer rows

test_cases.natural_language: Which genres have generated more revenue than the average genre?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT g.Name AS genre, ROUND(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 2) AS revenue FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY g.GenreId, g.Name HAVING SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity) > (SELECT AVG(genre_rev) FROM (SELECT SUM(il2.UnitPrice * il2.Quantity) AS genre_rev FROM Genre g2 JOIN Track t2 ON g2.GenreId = t2.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine il2 ON t2.TrackId = il2.TrackId GROUP BY g2.GenreId)) ORDER BY revenue DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: HAVING + nested subquery computing average of aggregates. Classic adversarial pattern.

test_cases.natural_language: For each support representative, how many customers do they handle and what is the total revenue from those customers?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.FirstName, e.LastName, COUNT(DISTINCT c.CustomerId) AS customer_count, ROUND(SUM(i.Total), 2) AS total_revenue FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY e.EmployeeId ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Requires COUNT(DISTINCT) to avoid inflating customer count due to multiple invoices per customer

test_cases.natural_language: What percentage of total revenue does each country contribute?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT BillingCountry AS country, ROUND(SUM(Total), 2) AS country_revenue, ROUND(SUM(Total) * 100.0 / (SELECT SUM(Total) FROM Invoice), 2) AS revenue_percentage FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry ORDER BY country_revenue DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Percentage calculation requires a scalar subquery for the total. Common failure point.

test_cases.natural_language: How many albums does each artist have, but only show artists with more than 2 albums?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ar.Name AS artist, COUNT(a.AlbumId) AS album_count FROM Artist ar JOIN Album a ON ar.ArtistId = a.ArtistId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name HAVING COUNT(a.AlbumId) > 2 ORDER BY album_count DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: HAVING clause filtering — models sometimes put the condition in WHERE instead of HAVING

test_cases.natural_language: What is the average number of tracks per album?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(track_count), 2) AS avg_tracks_per_album FROM (SELECT AlbumId, COUNT(*) AS track_count FROM Track GROUP BY AlbumId);
test_cases.adversarial_note: Average of aggregates — requires a subquery. Models often do AVG(COUNT(*)) which is invalid SQL.

test_cases.natural_language: Which customers have spent more than the average customer?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, ROUND(SUM(i.Total), 2) AS total_spent FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId HAVING SUM(i.Total) > (SELECT AVG(customer_total) FROM (SELECT SUM(Total) AS customer_total FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId)) ORDER BY total_spent DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Average of per-customer totals, not average of individual invoices. Very common mistake.

test_cases.natural_language: Rank the top 3 selling genres in each country by number of tracks sold
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT country, genre, tracks_sold, genre_rank FROM (SELECT i.BillingCountry AS country, g.Name AS genre, COUNT(il.InvoiceLineId) AS tracks_sold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY i.BillingCountry ORDER BY COUNT(il.InvoiceLineId) DESC) AS genre_rank FROM Invoice i JOIN InvoiceLine il ON i.InvoiceId = il.InvoiceId JOIN Track t ON il.TrackId = t.TrackId JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId GROUP BY i.BillingCountry, g.GenreId, g.Name) WHERE genre_rank <= 3 ORDER BY country, genre_rank;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Window function ROW_NUMBER + PARTITION BY + multi-table JOIN. Maximum difficulty.

test_cases.natural_language: Which genres have never been sold to any customer?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT g.Name AS genre FROM Genre g WHERE g.GenreId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT t.GenreId FROM Track t JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId WHERE t.GenreId IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY g.Name;
test_cases.adversarial_note: NOT IN with subquery + NULL safety. Alternative: NOT EXISTS or EXCEPT.

test_cases.natural_language: Find customers who have made purchases in every year that has invoice data
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT STRFTIME('%Y', i.InvoiceDate)) = (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT STRFTIME('%Y', InvoiceDate)) FROM Invoice) ORDER BY c.LastName;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Universal quantification via COUNT(DISTINCT) = total. Tests relational division pattern.

test_cases.natural_language: Show each customer's most recent invoice date and the total amount of that invoice
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, i.InvoiceDate AS most_recent_date, i.Total AS invoice_total FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId WHERE i.InvoiceDate = (SELECT MAX(i2.InvoiceDate) FROM Invoice i2 WHERE i2.CustomerId = c.CustomerId) ORDER BY most_recent_date DESC LIMIT 15;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Correlated subquery — must match each customer to THEIR latest invoice, not global max.

test_cases.natural_language: shwo me the top sellin artsts and how much there albums made
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ar.Name AS artist, COUNT(DISTINCT a.AlbumId) AS album_count, ROUND(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 2) AS total_revenue FROM Artist ar JOIN Album a ON ar.ArtistId = a.ArtistId JOIN Track t ON a.AlbumId = t.AlbumId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 10;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Heavy misspelling + requires 4-table JOIN + COUNT(DISTINCT) for albums

test_cases.natural_language: trakcs that nobdy has purchsed
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT t.Name AS track_name FROM Track t WHERE t.TrackId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT TrackId FROM InvoiceLine) ORDER BY t.Name LIMIT 20;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Heavy misspelling + negation (NOT IN subquery). Returns first 20 of 1519 unpurchased tracks.

test_cases.natural_language: gimme all rock n metal songs with there lenghts in minuts
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT t.Name AS track_name, g.Name AS genre, ROUND(t.Milliseconds / 60000.0, 2) AS duration_minutes FROM Track t JOIN Genre g ON t.GenreId = g.GenreId WHERE g.Name IN ('Rock', 'Metal') ORDER BY g.Name, t.Name LIMIT 20;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Informal language + multi-value filter + unit conversion (ms->min)

test_cases.natural_language: employes who dont manege anyone
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.FirstName, e.LastName, e.Title FROM Employee e WHERE e.EmployeeId NOT IN (SELECT DISTINCT ReportsTo FROM Employee WHERE ReportsTo IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY e.LastName;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Misspelling + self-referential NOT IN on Employee.ReportsTo. Must handle NULL in ReportsTo.

test_cases.natural_language: avg invioce amnt per counrty sortd high to low
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT BillingCountry AS country, ROUND(AVG(Total), 2) AS avg_invoice FROM Invoice GROUP BY BillingCountry ORDER BY avg_invoice DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Every word is misspelled. Tests pure NLP recovery ability.

test_cases.natural_language: 哪些藝人的專輯數量超過三張？列出藝人名稱和專輯數
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT ar.Name AS artist, COUNT(a.AlbumId) AS album_count FROM Artist ar JOIN Album a ON ar.ArtistId = a.ArtistId GROUP BY ar.ArtistId, ar.Name HAVING COUNT(a.AlbumId) > 3 ORDER BY album_count DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Chinese + HAVING clause + JOIN. Must translate '超過三張' to '> 3' not '>= 3'.

test_cases.natural_language: 売上金額が最も高い上位5ジャンルを教えてください
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT g.Name AS genre, ROUND(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 2) AS total_sales FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId GROUP BY g.GenreId, g.Name ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 5;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Japanese + multi-table aggregation. 'Top 5 genres by sales amount'.

test_cases.natural_language: Welche Kunden haben insgesamt mehr als 45 Dollar ausgegeben?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.FirstName, c.LastName, ROUND(SUM(i.Total), 2) AS total_spent FROM Customer c JOIN Invoice i ON c.CustomerId = i.CustomerId GROUP BY c.CustomerId HAVING SUM(i.Total) > 45 ORDER BY total_spent DESC;
test_cases.adversarial_note: German + HAVING on aggregate + JOIN. 'mehr als 45 Dollar ausgegeben' = 'spent more than $45'.

test_cases.natural_language: 找出每位員工負責的客戶中，消費總額最高的客戶是誰
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.FirstName AS emp_first, e.LastName AS emp_last, c.FirstName AS cust_first, c.LastName AS cust_last, ROUND(cs.total_spent, 2) AS total_spent FROM Employee e JOIN Customer c ON e.EmployeeId = c.SupportRepId JOIN (SELECT CustomerId, SUM(Total) AS total_spent FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId) cs ON c.CustomerId = cs.CustomerId WHERE cs.total_spent = (SELECT MAX(cs2.total_spent) FROM Customer c2 JOIN (SELECT CustomerId, SUM(Total) AS total_spent FROM Invoice GROUP BY CustomerId) cs2 ON c2.CustomerId = cs2.CustomerId WHERE c2.SupportRepId = e.EmployeeId) ORDER BY e.LastName;
test_cases.adversarial_note: Chinese + correlated subquery + per-group maximum. Among the hardest SQL patterns.

test_cases.natural_language: Quels sont les 3 genres les plus vendus en France ?
test_cases.ground_truth_sql: SELECT g.Name AS genre, ROUND(SUM(il.UnitPrice * il.Quantity), 2) AS total_sales FROM Genre g JOIN Track t ON g.GenreId = t.GenreId JOIN InvoiceLine il ON t.TrackId = il.TrackId JOIN Invoice i ON il.InvoiceId = i.InvoiceId WHERE i.BillingCountry = 'France' GROUP BY g.GenreId, g.Name ORDER BY total_sales DESC LIMIT 3;
test_cases.adversarial_note: French + 4-table JOIN + country filter + LIMIT. 'vendus en France' must filter by BillingCountry.
